    *   *Simplified:* 竞争
    *   *Traditional:* 競爭
    *   *競 (jìng):*
        *   Traditional form: 競
        *   Components: Two 言 (lời nói) on top, two 兄 (anh em) on the bottom. Actually, according to Shuowen Jiezi, 競 is formed by two 誩 (yán, arguing/talking) or two 言 on top of two 兄 (brothers). It originally depicted two brothers speaking/arguing loudly against each other, striving for dominance. *Correction for modern understanding:* It's two people (兄) speaking (言) against each other. So: 言 (lời nói) + 兄 (anh trai) x2.
    *   *爭 (zhēng):*
        *   Traditional form: 爭
        *   Components: 爫 (móng vuốt/tay với đồ vật) + 彐 (hay something pulling) + 亅 (dây/móc). Shuowen says it's two hands (top 爫 and right part of bottom) pulling a single object (the middle vertical stroke 亅 and the horizontal 彐). It represents pulling something back and forth -> fighting/contending.
    *   *Simplification process:*
        *   競 -> 竞 (Removed the top 言 parts, kept the bottom "like" 兄 but changed slightly to 立 on top + 儿 on bottom, though originally it was just a structural simplification. Wait, 竞 is derived from the cursive form of 競. The top becomes 立, bottom is 儿. The two 言 are completely dropped).
        *   爭 -> 争 (The top right claw 爫 is dropped, leaving ⺈ + 彐 + 亅. Actually, it's a simplified structural variant).
